title: coussapoa ferruginea
intro: Coussapoa ferruginea is a plant species in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the "Ironwood Vine" due to its reddish-brown bark. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Coussapoa ferruginea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Coussapoa ferruginea is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of tinctures or extracts, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine.
